display.purpose: Product title and rare screen-level headings.

body.purpose: Readable UI copy and panel explanations.

label.purpose: Stat labels, prompt metadata, and compact machine-readable labels.

prompt.purpose: Command bars and highlighted prompt mirrors.

shadows.name: workbench
shadows.purpose: Anchors the main composer or large work surfaces.

shadows.name: soft
shadows.purpose: Quiet lift for panels and logo surfaces.

shadows.name: overlay
shadows.purpose: Menus, popovers, skip link, and modals.

motion.name: state-fast
motion.purpose: Hover, focus, and color state transitions.

zIndex.name: content
zIndex.purpose: Local layered content above mirrors and backgrounds.

zIndex.name: popover
zIndex.purpose: Help bubbles and floating inspection panels.

zIndex.name: toast
zIndex.purpose: Transient notifications above modals when needed.

components.name: Icon Button
components.description: Compact toolbar action with neutral rest state and brass focus.
components.css: .ds-icon-button { border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.12); background: #232323; color: #a8a39a; border-radius: 8px; min-height: 2rem; padding: 0 .625rem; font: 500 14px IBM Plex Sans, system-ui, sans-serif; transition: border-color 140ms ease, background-color 140ms ease, color 140ms ease, box-shadow 140ms ease; } .ds-icon-button:hover { border-color: rgba(214,168,95,.3); background: #2b2b2b; color: #f3f3f1; } .ds-icon-button:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(214,168,95,.08); }

components.name: Modifier Chip
components.description: Small reversible state toggle for modifiers and quick suggestions.
components.css: .ds-chip { border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.12); background: #232323; color: #a8a39a; border-radius: 8px; padding: .375rem .75rem; font: 400 14px IBM Plex Sans, system-ui, sans-serif; transition: border-color 140ms ease, background-color 140ms ease, color 140ms ease; } .ds-chip:hover { border-color: rgba(214,168,95,.3); color: #f3f3f1; } .ds-chip:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(214,168,95,.08); } .ds-chip-active { border-color: rgba(214,168,95,.3); background: rgba(214,168,95,.14); color: #f3f3f1; }

components.name: Panel Card
components.description: Standard bordered product panel for results and summaries.
components.css: .ds-panel { border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.12); background: #1c1c1c; color: #f3f3f1; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1rem; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.14); font-family: IBM Plex Sans, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-panel-title { color: #f3f3f1; font-size: .8125rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.25rem; letter-spacing: .01em; } .ds-panel p { color: #cbc7bf; margin: .5rem 0 0; font-family: IBM Plex Mono, monospace; }

components.name: Text Field
components.description: Standard searchable input with brass focus ring.
components.css: .ds-field { display: grid; gap: .25rem; color: #a8a39a; font: 400 14px IBM Plex Sans, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-field input { border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.12); background: #1c1c1c; color: #f3f3f1; border-radius: 8px; padding: .5rem .75rem; font: inherit; transition: border-color 140ms ease, background-color 140ms ease, box-shadow 140ms ease; } .ds-field input:hover { border-color: rgba(214,168,95,.3); background: #232323; } .ds-field input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: rgba(214,168,95,.3); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(214,168,95,.08); }

components.name: Tool Tabs
components.description: Peer workspace mode switcher for Damage and Speed.
components.css: .ds-tabs { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: .375rem; font-family: IBM Plex Sans, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-tab { border: 1px solid transparent; color: #a8a39a; border-radius: 8px; padding: .375rem .75rem; text-decoration: none; font-size: 14px; transition: border-color 140ms ease, background-color 140ms ease, color 140ms ease; } .ds-tab:hover { border-color: rgba(255,255,255,.12); background: #232323; color: #f3f3f1; } .ds-tab:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(214,168,95,.08); } .ds-tab-active { border-color: rgba(214,168,95,.3); background: rgba(214,168,95,.08); color: #f3f3f1; }

components.name: Command Composer
components.html:
AttackerGliscorxIncineroar
#gliscor !earthquake adamant x incineroar
Ready to calculate
components.css: .ds-composer { overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.12); background: #181818; color: #f3f3f1; border-radius: 10px; box-shadow: 0 10px 24px rgba(0,0,0,.22); font-family: IBM Plex Sans, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-composer:focus-within { border-color: rgba(214,168,95,.3); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(214,168,95,.08); } .ds-strip { display: flex; gap: .75rem; align-items: center; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.06); background: #1c1c1c; padding: .5rem 1rem; color: #7f7a72; font-size: 12px; } .ds-strip strong { color: #f3f3f1; font-family: IBM Plex Mono, monospace; font-weight: 500; } .ds-prompt { padding: 1rem; color: #cbc7bf; font: 400 .9375rem/1.75 IBM Plex Mono, monospace; } .ds-status { border-top: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.06); background: #232323; color: #a8a39a; padding: .75rem 1rem; font: 400 13px IBM Plex Mono, monospace; }

narrative.overview: Unburden looks like a serious competitive work surface: compact, warm, and built for rapid iteration under pressure. The interface should feel like a tactical editor where every control either clarifies the prompt, exposes an assumption, or moves the user toward a decision.
keyCharacteristics: Warm charcoal surfaces with a rare brass accent.
IBM Plex Sans for interface clarity and IBM Plex Mono for prompt/data trust.
Compact controls with explicit hover, focus, active, disabled, and selected states.
Tonal layering first, shadows only when depth or overlay behavior matters.
Motion for state feedback only, never decoration.

rules.name: The Brass Rarity Rule
rules.body: Brass Signal is not decoration. If more than one thing in a local region is brass, only the selected or decisive thing keeps it.

rules.name: The Warm Neutral Rule
rules.body: Neutrals must stay warm and tinted. Never introduce pure black, pure white, or cold blue-gray defaults.

rules.name: The Editor Type Rule
rules.body: Prompt text, data values, and stat labels use mono. Buttons, tabs, prose, and navigation use sans. Do not mix them for flavor.

rules.name: The Layer Before Shadow Rule
rules.body: Try surface color, border, and spacing before adding a shadow. Shadows mean elevation, not decoration.

rules.name: The Outcome Token Rule
rules.body: Speed and damage relation colors use domain tokens: --outcome-favorable, --outcome-unfavorable, --outcome-tie, and --outcome-neutral. Do not style battle relations directly with generic --success or --danger unless the relation token does not exist.

rules.name: The Type Token Rule
rules.body: Pokemon type badges use --type-* CSS variables through getPokemonTypeColor(). Do not reintroduce component-local hex maps for types.

dos: Do keep the interface prompt-first, not prompt-only.
Do use Brass Signal only for action, selection, focus, and decisive result emphasis.
Do keep Damage Calculator and Speed Benchmark visually peer-level inside one workspace.
Do respect reduced motion and keep transitions in the 120ms to 250ms range.
Do use labels or copy alongside color for damage outcome, legality, and Speed relation.
Do route new overlay, control, type, and outcome styling through existing semantic tokens before adding new values.
donts: Don't recreate old-school form-heavy Pokemon calculators where users hunt through long fields.
Don't use generic SaaS dashboards with decorative cards, hero metrics, vague productivity styling, or repeated icon-card grids.
Don't use flashy gamer or neon esports UI that competes with tactical work.
Don't use cute Pokedex toy UI that treats the data as decoration instead of decision support.
Don't make the interface prompt-only in a way that hides too much state or forces grammar memorization.
Don't make silent assumptions around abilities, activation conditions, forms, legality, or set values.
Don't use gradient text, glassmorphism, decorative blur, or colored side-stripe borders.
Don't invent a new button, chip, or field vocabulary for a single screen.
Don't add component-local color maps, arbitrary z-index values, or custom focus rings when design-system tokens already exist.
